frozen_components: Audio-Language Model encoder and decoder (pre-trained, evaluated as-is in zero-shot)
architecture.summary: The paper evaluates Audio-Language Models (ALMs) on a novel audio entailment task. ALMs are foundation models trained on audio-text pairs. The paper benchmarks existing ALMs in zero-shot and linear-probe settings, and proposes a two-stage pipeline ('caption-before-reason') where an ALM first generates a caption from audio, then uses that caption to reason about a hypothesis and classify the entailment relationship (entailment, neutral, or contradiction). Information flows from audio through the ALM's audio encoder and text decoder/reasoning components.

biblio.title: Audio Entailment: Assessing Deductive Reasoning for Audio Understanding
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: Audio-Language Models show deficiencies in logical reasoning on the audio entailment task
caption-before-reason improves Zero-Shot performance by an absolute 6%
caption-before-reason improves linear-probe performance by an absolute 3%
Audio entailment task assesses whether a text hypothesis of audio content can be deduced from an audio recording, with three possible conclusions: entailment, neutral, or contradiction
